reason: Enabling FIPS mode requires adding a kernel boot parameter (fips=1) and changing the system cryptographic policy. On an already installed RHEL 9 system this is unsupported and can render the system unbootable, causing loss of console/SSH access. The policy explicitly classifies FIPS changes as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; authentication/authorization affected; potential boot failure; loss of SSH/console access; incompatible cryptographic libraries; system instability
